title: Readline Promises, Stream Injection, EOF, Abort, and Close Ownership · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript Pokedex interaction contains one plausible readline, token, type, validation, URL, fetch, promise, cache, abort, persistence, security, test, package, or release defect; find the first runtime or compile-time boundary failure before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in injected abortable readline session adapter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Create one readline promises interface from injected input and output and distinguish interface ownership from stream ownership.
Pass an AbortSignal to pending questions, classify abort separately, and remove or one-shot listeners without leaks.
Handle EOF, close, error, quit, and repeated close idempotently while preserving an explicit final session outcome.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Pokedex Ts Readline Behavior Tests Pokedex product engineering and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

content.text: A debug TypeScript team handles case 2291 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one readline interface, abortable questions, EOF handling, ownership, and idempotent close; each Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, and handle needs explicit ownership; then reorder one Promise, waiter, cache, timeout, or queue settlement and exclude stale state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use controlled readable and writable streams to prove prompts, commands, EOF, abort, error, close, and post-close behavior.

steps.instruction: Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-ts-readline-behavior-tests-50u81b" that uses case 8506 to prove one readline interface, abortable questions, EOF handling, ownership, and idempotent close.
